$doc: Board/module registry for the ESP32OS embedded workflow. Source of truth for the designer board selector AND the generated PlatformIO envs. One object per physical board/module. See board_registry.py for the loader + schema enforcement and feat_board_registry.md for the full schema spec. To add a board: append an object to `boards`, keep `id` unique and a valid PlatformIO env identifier (^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$), set `platformio_board` to a board id present in `python -m platformio boards`, and (if it has a display) set `display_profile` to a key from ui_designer.HARDWARE_PROFILES so the designer canvas matches the panel. Non-display modules MUST set has_display=false and omit display_profile.

boards.label: ESP32-S3-DevKitM-1 (reference, 256x128 Gray4 SSD1363)
boards.notes: Project reference target. Keeps the existing default SSD1363 256x128 Gray4 panel. Pins reflect the firmware's default DISPLAY_I2C_* / UART config; ws2812 is the on-board RGB on GPIO48 (WS2812 on most S3 DevKitM-1).

boards.label: M5StickC-Plus (ESP32-PICO, ST7789 135x240, AXP192)
boards.notes: ESP32-PICO-D4. PlatformIO board id `m5stick-c` covers both StickC and StickC-Plus (same SoC/flash); the Plus differs by its 1.14in ST7789v2 240x135 panel + AXP192 PMIC + MPU6886 IMU + buzzer, all selected via build_flags. AXP192 MUST be brought up over I2C (0x34) before the TFT backlight; without it the screen stays dark. IR LED on GPIO9, passive buzzer on GPIO2, Grove port I2C on 32/33. Source: github.com/m5stack/M5_Hardware (StickC-Plus).

boards.label: M5Stamp-C6LoRa (ESP32-C6 + SX1262, NO display, RGB LED)
boards.notes: Filip's real "C6L" prop module. ESP32-C6 (RISC-V, WiFi6/BLE5/802.15.4) + Semtech SX1262 LoRa transceiver. NO display panel at all -> has_display=false, the UI designer does NOT apply; this entry exists purely to carry pins/peripherals/backend config and a buildable headless firmware env (ESPOS_NO_DISPLAY). PlatformIO has no dedicated M5Stamp-C6 board, so it uses the generic `esp32-c6-devkitm-1` ESP32-C6 board (same SoC, 4MB flash) -- this is the honest, pio-valid choice rather than a fabricated board id. SX1262 wired over SPI; single WS2812 on GPIO8. LoRa pin map is the typical Stamp-C6LoRa layout; confirm against the specific unit's silk before flashing radio firmware. Source: github.com/m5stack/M5_Hardware (Stamp-C6LoRa).

boards.label: M5StickC original (ESP32-PICO, ST7735 80x160, AXP192)
boards.notes: ESP32-PICO-D4 StickC original. PlatformIO board id `m5stick-c` is the dedicated StickC entry and was verified in espressif32@6.12.0. Physical LCD is 80x160 portrait ST7735S; registry uses the existing landscape `tft_160x80_st7735` designer profile and records native portrait orientation. AXP192 supplies display/backlight rails and must be brought up over I2C before the TFT. IMU revisions may be MPU6886 or SH200Q on older units. Source: docs.m5stack.com/en/core/M5StickC.

boards.label: M5Stamp-S3 (ESP32-S3, NO display, RGB LED)
boards.notes: ESP32-S3FN8 Stamp module with no display panel, one WS2812B-2020 RGB LED on GPIO21, and download/user button on GPIO0. Dedicated PlatformIO board id `m5stack-stamps3` was verified in espressif32@6.12.0. Source: docs.m5stack.com/en/core/StampS3.

boards.label: M5AtomS3 (ESP32-S3, 128x128 GC9107 LCD, profile pending)
boards.notes: ESP32-S3 AtomS3 with 128x128 GC9107 color LCD and MPU6886 IMU. Dedicated PlatformIO board id `m5stack-atoms3` was verified in espressif32@6.12.0. `HARDWARE_PROFILES` has no matching 128x128 RGB LCD profile (the existing 128x128 key is 1bpp SH1107 OLED), so this entry is intentionally marked has_display=false until a real GC9107 profile exists. Source: docs.m5stack.com/en/core/AtomS3.

boards.label: M5Atom-Lite (ESP32-PICO, NO display, RGB LED)
boards.notes: ESP32-PICO-D4 Atom Lite with no display panel. PlatformIO board id `m5stack-atom` covers the Atom Lite/Matrix family and was verified in espressif32@6.12.0. Official Atom Lite hardware has one SK6812/WS2812-compatible RGB LED on GPIO27; the 5x5 RGB matrix is Atom Matrix hardware on the same shared PlatformIO id, not this Lite entry. Source: docs.m5stack.com/en/core/ATOM%20Lite.

boards.label: Espressif ESP32-C6-DevKitC-1 (reference, NO display, RGB LED)
boards.notes: Generic Espressif ESP32-C6-DevKitC-1 target, distinct from the M5Stamp-C6LoRa registry entry. Dedicated PlatformIO board id `esp32-c6-devkitc-1` was verified in espressif32@6.12.0. The on-board addressable RGB LED is GPIO8 and UART0 defaults to GPIO16/GPIO17. Source: docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-dev-kits/en/latest/esp32c6/esp32-c6-devkitc-1/user_guide.html.

boards.label: ESP32-WROOM-32E/UE Dev Module (classic esp32dev, NO display)
boards.notes: Classic ESP32-WROOM-32E/32UE dev-module target using PlatformIO `esp32dev`, verified in espressif32@6.12.0. GPIO1/GPIO3 are UART0, GPIO21/GPIO22 are the common I2C defaults, and GPIO18/19/23/5 are the common VSPI defaults. GPIO2 LED is common on many ESP32 DevKitC-style modules but not guaranteed across clones. Source: docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-dev-kits/en/latest/esp32/esp32-devkitc/user_guide.html.

boards.label: ESP32-C5 BLE LED stage prop (headless, pioarduino toolchain)
boards.notes: Filip's physical ESP32-C5 prop board (BLE-driven LED stage prop, on COM5). ISOLATED platform entry: the project-pinned espressif32@6.12.0 has NO ESP32-C5 board (verified: `pio boards --json-output espressif32` returns 0 C5 boards), so this single board pins its OWN newer toolchain -- the community pioarduino platform-espressif32 fork release 55.03.38-1 (frozen release-zip URL, reproducible), whose board DB ships `esp32-c5-devkitc-1` (mcu esp32c5, espidf+arduino, 4MB, no PSRAM). This does NOT change the platform/version of any other board or the global [env]/[esp32_base]; board_registry.render_pio_block only emits a per-board `platform =` line when it differs from BASE_PLATFORM, so every other generated env stays byte-identical and inherits espressif32@6.12.0 unchanged. Headless: no display panel -> has_display=false, the UI designer does not apply; ESPOS_NO_DISPLAY/ESPOS_HEADLESS keep the firmware screenless. Single WS2812 RGB on GPIO8 (DevKitC-1 default RGB LED). ESP32-C5 is RISC-V with WiFi6 + BLE5 + 802.15.4 (dual-band 2.4/5GHz); UART0 default is GPIO11(TX)/GPIO12(RX) and BOOT button GPIO28 on the C5-DevKitC-1 -- confirm GPIO assignments against the specific prop's wiring before driving the WS2812 string. Platform source: github.com/pioarduino/platform-espressif32 (release 55.03.38-1); board source: pioarduino boards/esp32-c5-devkitc-1.json; Espressif ESP32-C5-DevKitC-1 user guide.
